title: Eunkyung Yi
text:
Eunkyung Yi





Associate Professor
Dept of English Education
Ewha Womans University
Seoul, South Korea







I’m an associate professor in the Department of English Education at Ewha Womans University in Seoul, South Korea. Born and raised in Seoul, I was always in love with foreign language learning, which eventually evolved into a deeper interest in language and how it works. I completed my BA in English Language and Literature at Seoul National University and went on to work in industry, though it did not take long to realize that academia is where I truly belong. I returned to my home university for my MA in English Linguistics, then went on to complete my PhD under the supervision of Prof. Jean-Pierre Koenig at the State University of New York at Buffalo. I taught at Seoul National University before joining Ewha in 2021. My research interests include online sentence planning, the role of lexical information in sentence and discourse processing, and crosslinguistic differences in language processes.

Yi, Eunkyung (2006). The fake reflexive resultative construction in Korean. Korean Journal of English Language and Linguistics 6(1), 135–154.
